para_trans: Officers of the Tehsildar Cadre
On transfer
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Govt. AGS No.Transfer - 2026 / P.No.160 / Aastha-3
(E-Office No. 1640674)
Madam Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai 400 032
Reference: - The Maharashtra Regulation of Transfer of Government Employees and Prevention of Delays in the discharge of Government Duties Act, 2005
The Government Court: -
Regulation of transfers and discharge of government duties of Maharashtra Government employees
Section 4 (4) and 4 (5) of the Prevention of Delay in Dismantling Act, 2005
As per the provisions, the officers of the Tehsildar cadre mentioned below are required to present their names in front of Column No. Capable of the position shown in 4
in the interest of public service and administrative convenience with the approval of the authorities.
The position is being given.
2. The said order comes into immediate effect and the said officers are being exonerated of their duties unilaterally under this order. Mr. Arvind Hinge,
The Tehsildar should immediately join the place of posting given to him.

The officer concerned shall take care of joining the place of posting within the prescribed period as per the rules of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Period of assumption of office, Non-Service and Provisions during periods of suspension, dismissal and removal from service) Rules, 1981. Those concerned should note that otherwise, if they fail to join the post of office bearer, the period of their absence will be treated as "non-working day" (7 | & 5/70/7).
table_trans: Tehsildar (General Administration) Divisional Commissioner Office, Nagpur

para_trans: 4. Leave of the officer concerned for any reason without prior permission of the Government.
should not be approved. Also, as per Rule-30 (c) of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1981
No leave applications of officers transferred by the original office shall be entertained.
If such applications are received, they are returned by post to the concerned authorities at the original address.
should do.
(5) The officer concerned shall not, in accordance with the said order, immediately enter upon the office of the incumbent.
In the event of or attempt to cancel the transfer under any kind of pressure, this action
For violating Rule 23 of the Maharashtra Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1979,
Disciplinary action will be taken against them considering it as misconduct.
The concerned should take note.
G. Sadar Jhasan Biya www.maharashtra.gov.in of the Government of Maharashtra
